message:
feat(version): Add tests for version detection module (bd-1jt.1.7)

- Add comprehensive test suite for src/version.sh
- Tests cover Rust, Go, Node.js, and Python version detection
- Tests verify tag creation, dry-run, and JSON output
- All 12 tests pass

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.4","title":"Implement dsr fallback command - trigger local build pipeline","description":"# Implement `dsr fallback` — Orchestrated Build + Release

## Purpose
End-to-end fallback pipeline when GH Actions is throttled: build, verify, release, and report.

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr fallback <tool>
 dsr fallback <tool> --version v1.2.3
 dsr fallback <tool> --build-only
 dsr fallback <tool> --skip-checks
 dsr fallback <tool> --dry-run
 dsr fallback <tool> --resume
```

## Flow (must be deterministic)
1. Resolve repo config + tag/commit (build from tag)
2. Run pre-release checks (unless --skip-checks)
3. Acquire lock + create run_id
4. Build all targets (act + native hosts)
5. Generate manifest + checksums + signatures
6. Verify artifacts
7. Create release and upload assets
8. Emit JSON summary + status report

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Works end-to-end without manual intervention
- [ ] Uses manifest for release validation
- [ ] Supports resume after partial failure
- [ ] Clear logs + JSON output (schema-compliant)
- [ ] --dry-run outputs a deterministic plan without side  {"id":"bd-1jt.1.5","title":"Create default repos.d registry with all 16 tools configured","description":"# Create Default repos.d Registry with All 16 Tools Configured

## Purpose
Ship a complete, ready-to-use repository registry so `dsr` can build/release/install the full Dicklesworthstone toolchain without manual setup.

## Why this matters
Most commands (`check`, `build`, `release`, installers) rely on accurate repo metadata. Missing or inconsistent entries cause brittle behavior and user confusion.

## Location
`~/.config/dsr/repos.d/` (created by `dsr config init`)
- One file per tool: `repos.d/<tool>.yaml`
- Each file contains `schema_version` + tool fields

## Required Fields (per tool)
- `repo` (owner/name)
- `local_path` (absolute path, usually under `/data/projects`, but allow overrides)
- `language` (rust|go|bun|node)
- `binary_name`
- `build_cmd` (reproducible, non-interactive)
- `targets` (os/arch list)
- `checks` (lint/test/typecheck commands)
- `workflow` + `act_job_map`
- `artifact_naming` + `archive_format`
- `enabled` (bool) to allow opt-out

## Guardrails
- Never create new directories under `/data/projects`
- Missing `local_path` → warn, do not mutate
- Use `yq` for safe writes when available; read-only fallback otherwise

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 16 tools configured with full metadata
- [ ] Paths validated (existence check + absolute)
- [ ] Workflows + job mappings verified
- [ ] Artifact naming matches installer expectations
- [ ] Registry lives in `repos.d/` (no monolithic `repos.yaml`)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:14:13.167130536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:45:29.294176214Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:45:29.293673988Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:34:33.023057790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.6","title":"Implement notification system for build status","description":"# Notification System for Build Status

## Purpose
Notify users when builds complete/fail or need attention, without leaking secrets or blocking automation.

## Channels
- Terminal (default, stderr)
- Agent Mail (MCP) when available
- Slack webhook (optional)
- Email (optional, future)

## Design Requirements
- **Non-interactive safe**: no prompts when `--non-interactive`
- **Redaction**: tokens/secrets never appear in notifications or logs
- **Deduplication** by `run_id` (one notification per state transition)
- **Structured log events** so notifications are traceable

## Agent Mail Integration (Best Practice)
- Use MCP `send_message` when available
- Fall back gracefully if server not running
- Provide a short summary + path to logs/manifests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Terminal notifications on macOS/Linux
- [ ] Agent Mail integration works when available
- [ ] Slack/email optional and safe
- [ ] Notifications are non-blocking and logged
- [ ] Duplicate alerts suppressed per run_id","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:24:21.269569798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:26:00.097906097Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:02.732813620Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:34:42.109646409Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.1.7","title":"Implement auto-tag from version files","description":"# Auto-Tag from Version Files

## Purpose
Automatically create git tags when version files (Cargo.toml, package.json, VERSION, etc.) are updated. This keeps releases consistent across tools and avoids manual tagging errors.

## Version Sources (configurable)
| Language | File | Pattern |
|----------|------|---------|
| Rust | Cargo.toml | version = "X.Y.Z" |
| Go | VERSION or version.go | Version = "X.Y.Z" |
| Bun/Node | package.json | "version": "X.Y.Z" |
| Python | pyproject.toml | version = "X.Y.Z" |

## Implementation Notes
- Use repo registry (`repos.d/<tool>.yaml`) to resolve `local_path`
- **No global cd** — use `git -C <repo>`
- Dirty tree detection via exit codes (`git diff-index --quiet HEAD --`)
- Tag existence via `git show-ref --tags -d "refs/tags/vX.Y.Z"`
- Support `--dry-run` and `--non-interactive`

## dsr Command
```bash
dsr version detect <tool>   # Show current version
dsr version tag <tool>      # Create tag if needed
dsr version tag --all       # Tag all repos with new versions
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detect version from all supported language files
- [ ] Skip if tag already exists
- [ ] Create annotated tags
- [ ] Push tags to remote
- [ ] All git operations use `git -C` (no global cd)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:28:57.429934130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:53:52.212718153Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:48.309988979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:34:54.300293741Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.8","title":"Implement dsr config command and configuration management","description":"# dsr Configuration Management (XDG + Schema Validation)

## Purpose
Centralize configuration so every command uses the same sources of truth for repos, hosts, secrets, and defaults. Consistent config prevents drift and makes automation reproducible.

## XDG Layout
- `~/.config/dsr/`
  - `config.yaml` (global defaults + schema_version)
  - `repos.yaml` (tool registry + build matrix)
  - `hosts.yaml` (build hosts + capabilities)
  - `notifications.yaml`
- `~/.cache/dsr/` (API cache, downloads)
- `~/.local/state/dsr/` (logs, state, run history)

## Precedence Rules
1. CLI flags
2. Environment variables (`DSR_*`)
3. Config files
4. Built-in defaults

## repos.yaml Schema (minimum fields)
- `tool`, `repo`, `local_path`
- `language`, `build_cmd`, `binary_name`
- `targets` (os/arch list)
- `checks` (pre-release gates)
- `workflow` + `act_job_map`
- `artifact_naming` + `archive_format`

## hosts.yaml Schema
- host aliases, platform, capabilities, concurrency limits
- ssh options (connect timeout, identity)

## Migration & Versioning
- `schema_version` required
- `dsr config migrate` upgrades older schemas
- Unknown keys rejected unless `--allow-unknown`

## Commands
```bash
dsr config init
 dsr config show [key]
 dsr config validate
 dsr config set KEY=VALUE
 dsr config edit
 dsr config migrate
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config loads at startup with deterministic precedence
- [ ] Validation catches missing/invalid keys
- [ ] Defaults are sane and documented
- [ ] `--json` outputs resolved config for automation
- [ ] yq-based edits are safe; fallback mode is read-only","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:23:27.935807062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:57:12.840449840Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T15:47:07.588658283Z","close_reason":"Implemented dsr config command with init/show/get/set/validate/edit subcommands. Created src/config.sh module and main dsr CLI. XDG-compliant paths, JSON output support, precedence rules (CLI > env > config > defaults). Also implemented dsr doctor for diagnostics.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:33:56.274007404Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:38:53.251178068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-8cg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:50:48.532928914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.9","title":"Implement GitHub API adapter with caching + rate-limit handling","description":"# Implement GitHub API Adapter with Caching + Rate-Limit Handling

## Purpose
Centralize all GitHub API access so commands are consistent, cached, and resilient to rate limits.

## Responsibilities
- Prefer gh api when available (auth + pagination)
- Fallback to curl + GITHUB_TOKEN
- Support ETag / If-None-Match caching in ~/.cache/dsr/github/
- Normalize errors into a common JSON shape
- Expose helpers for workflow runs, releases, tags, and compare endpoints

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unified helper used by dsr check/watch/release
- [ ] API responses cached with TTL + ETag
- [ ] Rate-limit errors trigger backoff + clear messages
- [ ] All errors map to global exit codes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:31.418006660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T16:13:44.413349290Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T16:13:44.413268548Z","close_reason":"Implemented GitHub API adapter in src/github.sh with: gh_api() for unified API access (gh CLI or curl fallback), ETag caching in ~/.cache/dsr/github/, rate-limit handling with backoff, and helpers for workflow_runs, releases, tags, compare, and asset upload.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:31.418006660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2","title":"Theme 2: Local Build Infrastructure","description":"# Local Build Infrastructure

## Purpose
Build binaries locally when GH Actions is throttled, using available hardware:
- Linux builds on Threadripper workstation (trj, local)
- macOS builds on Mac mini (mmini)
- Windows builds on Windows laptop (wlap) or cross-compilation fallback

## Build Hosts

### Threadripper Workstation (trj)
- Connection: local
- OS: Ubuntu Linux
- Arch: x86_64
- Can build: Linux x86_64, Linux ARM64 (via cross), Windows (via cross)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun, Docker/act

### Mac mini (mmini)
- Connection: ssh mmini (via Tailscale)
- OS: macOS
- Arch: ARM64 (Apple Silicon)
- Can build: macOS ARM64 (native), macOS x86_64 (via arch -x86_64)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun

### Windows laptop (wlap)
- Connection: ssh wlap (via Tailscale)
- OS: Windows 10/11
- Arch: x86_64
- Can build: Windows x86_64 (native)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun

## Cross-Compilation Strategy
For targets we can't build natively:
- Rust: cargo-zigbuild or cross for Windows/ARM
- Go: GOOS/GOARCH environment variables
- Bun: bun compile with target flag

## Key Commands
```bash
dsr build ntm                       # Build for all targets
dsr build ntm --target linux-amd64  # Specific target
dsr build ntm --host trj            # Force specific build host
```

## Target Matrix (example)

| Tool | Language | Linux x64 | Linux ARM | macOS ARM | macOS x64 | Windows |
|------|----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|---------|
| ntm  | Go       | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| cass | Rust     | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| bv   | Go       | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| ...  | ...      | ...       | ...       | ...       | ...       | ...     |

## Build Output
Artifacts + logs live under the state directory:
- `~/.local/state/dsr/artifacts/<tool>/<version>/<target>/`
- `~/.local/state/dsr/builds/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>/` (per-host logs + metadata)

## Dependencies
- SSH access to build hosts (mmini, wlap)
- Toolchains installed on each host
- Docker + act available on trj for Linux workflows
- Sufficient disk space (~5GB per full build)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Host names and mappings align with `hosts.yaml` (trj/mmini/wlap)
- [ ] Artifacts and logs land in state directory with a manifest
- [ ] Cross-compilation paths documented for Rust/Go/Bun
- [ ] Non-interactive builds run without prompts","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:17.777193563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:51:40.191796583Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:17.777193563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-8cg","title":"Update CLI contract + schemas for new commands","description":"# Update CLI Contract + Schemas for New Commands

## Purpose
Keep the CLI contract and JSON schemas authoritative as new commands/features land (status/report, prune, repos validate/discover, config migrate, fallback output).

## Scope
- Update `docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md` with new subcommands, flags, and example JSON payloads.
- Add/extend schema files:
  - `schemas/status-details.json`
  - `schemas/report-details.json` (or reuse status if identical)
  - `schemas/prune-details.json`
  - `schemas/repos-details.json`
  - `schemas/config-details.json`
  - `schemas/fallback-details.json`
- Ensure the envelope schema includes `schema_version` (additive) and stays backward compatible.
- Align error codes and stream-separation guidance for the new commands.

## Design Notes
- Prefer additive schema changes (no breaking removals).
- Keep names consistent (`status` vs `report`) across docs, schemas, and CLI help.
- Include explicit `run_id`, `duration_ms`, `warnings`, and `errors` fields in all new schemas.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CLI_CONTRACT documents status/report, prune, repos validate/discover, config validate/migrate, and fallback outputs.
- [ ] New schema files exist and validate against example fixtures.
- [ ] Schema changes are additive and compatible with existing envelope.
- [ ] Examples include both success and error cases for each new command.

## Test Plan
- [ ] Add fixtures for each new schema and validate with `test_json_schemas.sh` (ajv/jq paths).
- [ ] CI validates schema changes with strict checks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:49:46.083055419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T16:05:36.575837209Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T16:05:36.575789839Z","close_reason":"Created new schema files (status-details.json, prune-details.json, repos-details.json, config-details.json, fallback-details.json), updated envelope.json with new commands and schema_version field, created test fixtures, and updated CLI_CONTRACT.md with documentation for status, prune, repos validate/discover, and config migrate subcommands.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8cg","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:50:37.780347948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-9vb","title":"E2E integration test: dsr check command","description":"# E2E Integration Test: dsr check Command

## Command Purpose
`dsr check` detects throttled GitHub Actions workflow runs by monitoring queue times.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Basic Functionality
- `dsr check` with no repos returns helpful message
- `dsr check --repos ntm` checks specified repo
- `dsr check --all` checks all registered repos
- `dsr check --threshold 300` uses custom threshold

### 2. JSON Mode Output
- `dsr check --json` returns valid JSON
- JSON matches check-details.json schema
- Contains repos_checked, throttled, healthy arrays
- Exit code reflected in envelope

### 3. Exit Codes
- Exit 0 when no repos throttled
- Exit 1 when any repo throttled
- Exit 3 on gh auth failure
- Exit 4 on invalid arguments

### 4. Flag Combinations
- `--quiet` suppresses non-error output
- `--verbose` shows detailed API calls
- `--non-interactive` disables prompts
- `--json --quiet` outputs only JSON

### 5. Edge Cases
- Unknown repo name produces error
- Network failure handled gracefully
- Rate-limited API returns retry hint
- Empty workflow runs list handled

## Test Approach
- Use real dsr binary
- Test against a test repo OR skip network tests
- Verify JSON schema compliance
- Capture and verify exit codes
- Use temp config dir for isolation

## Logging Requirements
- Log test name and description
- Log command executed
- Log expected vs actual output
- Log pass/fail with timing","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:34:15.818315831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:12.865396967Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:46:34.938101688Z","close_reason":"CONSOLIDATED: Merged into bd-1jt.5.3 (integration tests for dsr commands)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9vb","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:34:15.818315831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9vb","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:12.864689495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-dy5","title":"E2E: dsr prune (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr prune (real behavior)

## Purpose
Verify prune respects safety rules and only deletes within allowed dsr state paths.

## Approach
- Use temp state dirs with dummy artifacts/logs.
- Run `dsr prune --dry-run` and real prune.
- Ensure safe delete refuses non-dsr paths.

## Logging
- Print prune plan and deleted paths.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr and directory tree.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dry-run lists intended deletions.
- [ ] Real prune removes only allowed files.
- [ ] --json output schema-valid and stderr clean on success.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:56:34.343178800Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:01.101622931Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dy5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:58.920513238Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dy5","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:01.100860484Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-f97","title":"Test harness: real-behavior logging + skip protocol","description":"# Test Harness: Real-Behavior Logging + Skip Protocol

## Purpose
Standardize how tests log commands, capture stdout/stderr, and skip when external deps are missing, without resorting to mocks.

## Scope
- Shared helpers that:
  - Capture stdout/stderr on failure.
  - Print the exact command and environment.
  - Emit a structured skip message with actionable install steps.
- Enforce stream separation checks in tests.

## Requirements
- All unit/E2E tests import the same harness helpers.
- Skips must include a **reason** and **next step** (e.g., "Install minisign" or "Run gh auth login").
- Never suppress errors silently.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A reusable helper exists for log capture + skip reasons.
- [ ] Sample tests show consistent logging format.
- [ ] Failures dump stdout/stderr and relevant env vars.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:54:37.594231147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:54:37.594231147Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-iu8","title":"Write README.md user guide for dsr","description":"# Write README.md User Guide for dsr

## Purpose
Provide a clear, single entry-point doc for users and operators so dsr can be installed, configured, and used without reading internal plans.

## Must Include
- What dsr is + when to use it (GH Actions throttling)
- Quickstart: `dsr config init`, `dsr repos list`, `dsr check`, `dsr build`, `dsr release`, `dsr fallback`, `dsr doctor`, `dsr status`
- Config + registry layout (XDG + `repos.d/`)
- Build host expectations (trj/mmini/wlap) + toolchains
- Installer strategy + security notes (checksums/minisign)
- Exit codes table
- Logging/state locations and how to inspect failures
- Non-interactive + JSON modes (stream separation)
- Links to `docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md` and `docs/ACT_SETUP.md`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] README exists and is consistent with CLI contract
- [ ] Examples are copy-pasteable and current
- [ ] No contradictions with docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md
- [ ] Links to docs and config locations are  {"id":"bd-ov2","title":"Test infrastructure: coverage reporting and metrics","description":"# Test Infrastructure: Coverage Reporting and Metrics

## Purpose
Provide function-level and line-level coverage estimation for Bash scripts without external tools.

## Approach
Since Bash doesn't have built-in coverage tools like gcov, we use:
1. **Function enumeration**: Parse exported functions from each module
2. **Test mapping**: Track which tests call which functions
3. **Execution tracing**: Optional PS4-based tracing for line coverage

## Coverage Metrics

### 1. Function Coverage
- List all exported functions per module
- Track function calls during test execution
- Report: `functions_tested / functions_total`

### 2. Test-to-Function Mapping
```json
{
  "config.sh": {
    "config_init": ["test_config.sh::test_init"],
    "config_load": ["test_config.sh::test_load", "test_e2e.sh::test_full_flow"],
    "config_validate": []  // Not tested\!
  }
}
```

### 3. Gap Analysis
- Functions with zero test coverage
- Functions with only happy-path coverage
- Modules with low overall coverage

## Implementation

### Coverage Script
```bash
./scripts/coverage.sh [--module config.sh] [--format json|text]
```

### Output
```
=== Coverage Report ===
Module            Functions  Tested  Coverage
config.sh              8       6     75%
github.sh             10       7     70%
build_state.sh        15      15    100%
...
TOTAL                 50      42     84%

Untested functions:
- config.sh: config_validate
- github.sh: gh_create_release, gh_upload_asset, gh_delete_release
```

## CI Integration
- Run after test suite
- Fail if coverage drops below threshold
- Generate JSON for trending

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parse exported functions from all src/*.sh
- [ ] Track function calls during test runs
- [ ] Generate human-readable report
- [ ] Generate JSON for CI
- [ ] Flag untested functions
- [ ] Optional threshold enforcement","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:27.277939425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:49:24.894461697Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ov2","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:27.277939425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,241 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# test_version.sh - Tests for src/version.sh
+#
+# Usage: ./scripts/tests/test_version.sh
+
+set -uo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
+
+# Source version module and dependencies
+source "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/logging.sh"
+source "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/git_ops.sh"
+source "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/version.sh"
+
+# Test counters
+TESTS_RUN=0
+TESTS_PASSED=0
+TESTS_FAILED=0
+
+# Colors
+if [[ -t 1 ]]; then
+    GREEN=$'\033[0;32m'
+    RED=$'\033[0;31m'
+    YELLOW=$'\033[0;33m'
+    NC=$'\033[0m'
+else
+    GREEN='' RED='' YELLOW='' NC=''
+fi
+
+pass() { ((TESTS_PASSED++)); echo "${GREEN}✓${NC} $1"; }
+fail() { ((TESTS_FAILED++)); echo "${RED}✗${NC} $1: $2"; }
+skip() { echo "${YELLOW}○${NC} $1 (skipped: $2)"; }
+
+run_test() {
+    local name="$1"
+    ((TESTS_RUN++))
+    echo "Running: $name"
+}
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Setup
+# ============================================================================
+
+TEMP_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
+trap 'rm -rf "$TEMP_DIR"' EXIT
+
+# Create a mock Rust project
+setup_rust_project() {
+    local dir="$TEMP_DIR/rust_project"
+    mkdir -p "$dir"
+    git -C "$dir" init -q
+    cat > "$dir/Cargo.toml" << 'EOF'
+[package]
+name = "test-tool"
+version = "1.2.3"
+edition = "2021"
+EOF
+    git -C "$dir" add .
+    git -C "$dir" commit -q -m "Initial commit"
+    echo "$dir"
+}
+
+# Create a mock Node.js project
+setup_node_project() {
+    local dir="$TEMP_DIR/node_project"
+    mkdir -p "$dir"
+    git -C "$dir" init -q
+    cat > "$dir/package.json" << 'EOF'
+{
+  "name": "test-tool",
+  "version": "2.0.0-beta.1"
+}
+EOF
+    git -C "$dir" add .
+    git -C "$dir" commit -q -m "Initial commit"
+    echo "$dir"
+}
+
+# Create a mock Python project
+setup_python_project() {
+    local dir="$TEMP_DIR/python_project"
+    mkdir -p "$dir"
+    git -C "$dir" init -q
+    cat > "$dir/pyproject.toml" << 'EOF'
+[project]
+name = "test-tool"
+version = "0.9.5"
+EOF
+    git -C "$dir" add .
+    git -C "$dir" commit -q -m "Initial commit"
+    echo "$dir"
+}
+
+# Create a mock Go project with VERSION file
+setup_go_project() {
+    local dir="$TEMP_DIR/go_project"
+    mkdir -p "$dir"
+    git -C "$dir" init -q
+    echo "3.1.4" > "$dir/VERSION"
+    git -C "$dir" add .
+    git -C "$dir" commit -q -m "Initial commit"
+    echo "$dir"
+}
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Tests
+# ============================================================================
+
+echo "========================================="
+echo "Testing src/version.sh"
+echo "========================================="
+echo ""
+
+# Test 1: Rust version detection
+run_test "version_detect - Rust (Cargo.toml)"
+rust_dir=$(setup_rust_project)
+version=$(version_detect "$rust_dir" 2>/dev/null)
+if [[ "$version" == "1.2.3" ]]; then
+    pass "Detected Rust version: $version"
+else
+    fail "Rust version" "expected 1.2.3, got '$version'"
+fi
+
+# Test 2: Node.js version detection
+run_test "version_detect - Node.js (package.json)"
+node_dir=$(setup_node_project)
+version=$(version_detect "$node_dir" 2>/dev/null)
+if [[ "$version" == "2.0.0-beta.1" ]]; then
+    pass "Detected Node.js version: $version"
+else
+    fail "Node.js version" "expected 2.0.0-beta.1, got '$version'"
+fi
+
+# Test 3: Python version detection
+run_test "version_detect - Python (pyproject.toml)"
+python_dir=$(setup_python_project)
+version=$(version_detect "$python_dir" 2>/dev/null)
+if [[ "$version" == "0.9.5" ]]; then
+    pass "Detected Python version: $version"
+else
+    fail "Python version" "expected 0.9.5, got '$version'"
+fi
+
+# Test 4: Go version detection (VERSION file)
+run_test "version_detect - Go (VERSION file)"
+go_dir=$(setup_go_project)
+version=$(version_detect "$go_dir" 2>/dev/null)
+if [[ "$version" == "3.1.4" ]]; then
+    pass "Detected Go version: $version"
+else
+    fail "Go version" "expected 3.1.4, got '$version'"
+fi
+
+# Test 5: version_needs_tag - no tag exists
+run_test "version_needs_tag - tag does not exist"
+if version_needs_tag "$rust_dir" 2>/dev/null; then
+    pass "Correctly reports tag needed"
+else
+    fail "version_needs_tag" "should return 0 when tag doesn't exist"
+fi
+
+# Test 6: version_needs_tag - tag exists
+run_test "version_needs_tag - tag exists"
+git -C "$rust_dir" tag v1.2.3
+if ! version_needs_tag "$rust_dir" 2>/dev/null; then
+    pass "Correctly reports tag not needed"
+else
+    fail "version_needs_tag" "should return 1 when tag exists"
+fi
+
+# Test 7: version_create_tag - dry-run
+run_test "version_create_tag - dry-run"
+output=$(version_create_tag "$node_dir" --dry-run 2>&1)
+if [[ "$output" == *"[DRY-RUN]"* && "$output" == *"v2.0.0-beta.1"* ]]; then
+    pass "Dry-run outputs correct info"
+else
+    fail "version_create_tag --dry-run" "missing expected output"
+fi
+
+# Test 8: version_create_tag - actual tag
+run_test "version_create_tag - creates tag"
+if version_create_tag "$node_dir" 2>/dev/null; then
+    if git -C "$node_dir" show-ref --tags --verify "refs/tags/v2.0.0-beta.1" &>/dev/null; then
+        pass "Tag v2.0.0-beta.1 created successfully"
+    else
+        fail "version_create_tag" "tag not found in repo"
+    fi
+else
+    fail "version_create_tag" "command failed"
+fi
+
+# Test 9: version_create_tag - tag already exists
+run_test "version_create_tag - already exists"
+output=$(version_create_tag "$node_dir" 2>&1)
+if [[ "$output" == *"already exists"* ]]; then
+    pass "Correctly handles existing tag"
+else
+    fail "version_create_tag" "should warn about existing tag"
+fi
+
+# Test 10: version_info_json
+run_test "version_info_json - returns valid JSON"
+json=$(version_info_json "$rust_dir")
+if echo "$json" | jq -e '.version == "1.2.3" and .tag == "v1.2.3"' &>/dev/null; then
+    pass "JSON output is valid and correct"
+else
+    fail "version_info_json" "invalid JSON or wrong values"
+fi
+
+# Test 11: Language-specific detection
+run_test "version_detect - language parameter"
+version=$(version_detect "$rust_dir" rust 2>/dev/null)
+if [[ "$version" == "1.2.3" ]]; then
+    pass "Language-specific detection works"
+else
+    fail "version_detect with language" "expected 1.2.3, got '$version'"
+fi
+
+# Test 12: Non-existent directory
+run_test "version_detect - non-existent directory"
+if ! version_detect "/nonexistent/path" 2>/dev/null; then
+    pass "Returns error for non-existent path"
+else
+    fail "version_detect" "should fail for non-existent directory"
+fi
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Summary
+# ============================================================================
+
+echo ""
+echo "========================================="
+echo "Results: $TESTS_PASSED/$TESTS_RUN passed"
+if [[ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]]; then
+    echo "${RED}$TESTS_FAILED test(s) failed${NC}"
+    exit 1
+else
+    echo "${GREEN}All tests passed!${NC}"
+    exit 0
+fi
